math_recheck: Re-derived symbolically (sympy) all three kBT/h routes (loose-vib ν cancellation incl. small-ν expansion kT/hν; slab one-sided mean forward speed sqrt(kT/2πm*) with the flux factor-of-1/2 and δ,m* cancellation; flux integral ∫0∞(p/m*)e^{-p²/2m*kT}dp=kT over Z1=sqrt(2πm*kT) giving kT/h), the collision-theory structureless reduction (TST result ∝ sqrt(kT/μ)·d² = σ⟨u_rel⟩ up to symmetry number), Ea=ΔH‡+RT/+2RT with A=e·/e²·(kBT/h)exp(ΔS‡/R), and isotope-effect sign (E0(D)>E0(H), k_H/k_D>1); confirmed central formula and verbatim Eyring flux quote against Bonnet arXiv:1603.01662 and Vallance notes; ran code/eyring_rate.py and both inline code blocks: kBT/h=6.25e12 s^-1 at 300K.
fixes: reasoning.md:15 - tightened '3N-1 ordinary bound modes' to '3N-1 ordinary bound degrees of freedom -- overall translation and rotation plus remaining real vibrations' so it is not misread as 3N-1 vibrational modes; clarified the count is out of the complex's 3N total DOF
caveat: Primary source (Eyring, J. Chem. Phys. 3, 107 (1935), DOI 10.1063/1.1749604) is paywalled with no clean public scan; load-bearing equations and Eyring's verbatim opening flux procedure were verified against faithful secondaries (Bonnet arXiv:1603.01662, Yi arXiv:physics/0102027, Vallance/Oxford notes), as documented in refs/SOURCES.md.
